# SESSION BRIEF · BU-post-hero-demo — Hero image on Post (demo path)

_Brief version: 1.0 · Author: Paul + Claude · Date: 2026-04-26_
_Priority: Phase 2 demo-leverage. Single-purpose BU. Renders posts
visually closer to real-world activist comms (per WhatsApp pattern
review 2026-04-26)._
_Pairs with: parking-lot entry "Direct image upload on Post — priority
bump from image-handling phased plan"; revises D046 phased plan to
permit a stub-upload demo path on the way to Phase 2 BU-image._

---

## Objective

A post can carry an optional hero image, chosen at compose time from a
fixed set of seeded demo images. The hero renders at the top of the
post card in the feed and at the top of the post detail page. No real
upload pipeline — the picker returns one of N seeded image URLs.

Success: Eddie composes a post, picks a hero image from a small grid,
publishes; the post appears in the feed with the chosen image at the
top of the card; clicking through to the detail page shows the same
image larger.

---

## Scope

### Build in this session

**Decision record (must land before schema migration):**

- `docs/architecture/decision-log.md` — append `D064 · Post.heroImageUrl
  optional field; demo-stub picker on top of D046's phased plan`
  (MODIFY, append-only)

**Schema:**

- `prisma/schema.prisma` (MODIFY — add `heroImageUrl String?` to the
  `Post` model. Place after `linkImageUrl` block. Do NOT modify any
  existing fields, indexes, relations, or other models.)
- `prisma/migrations/<timestamp>_add_post_hero_image_url/migration.sql`
  (generated by `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_post_hero_image_url`)

**Seeded image set:**

- `public/seed-images/` (new directory) — 6–8 royalty-free JPGs (e.g.
  Unsplash CC0 / Pexels free-license). Filenames: `01.jpg`–`08.jpg`.
  Each ≤ 400 KB, ≤ 1600px on long edge. Photos should feel
  generic-activist-friendly: protest banners, hands holding signs,
  urban scenes, etc. License attribution tracked in
  `public/seed-images/README.md`.
- `public/seed-images/README.md` (new) — sources, licenses, and the
  rule that this directory is for demo only and goes away when
  Phase 2 BU-image lands.
- `shared/seed-images.ts` (new) — exports `SEED_HERO_IMAGES: readonly
  { url: string; alt: string }[]` — the canonical demo bucket.
  Service and components both import from here.

**Validation:**

- `shared/validation/post.ts` (MODIFY — add optional `heroImageUrl:
  z.string().url().nullable().optional()` to the create schema. Must
  reject any URL not in `SEED_HERO_IMAGES` for demo safety: validator
  cross-references the seeded list. Do NOT change other fields.)

**Service:**

- `server/services/post.ts` (MODIFY — `createPost` accepts
  `heroImageUrl?: string | null`; `listPosts` and `getPostById` return
  it. Return shape gains `heroImageUrl` as a nullable string at the
  end of the existing object — do NOT reorder existing fields. Add
  `// BU-post-hero-demo (D064)` comment near the field.)

**Router:**

- `server/routers/post.ts` (MODIFY — `create` mutation input passes
  through `heroImageUrl`. No new procedures.)

**Composer UI:**

- `components/HeroImagePicker.tsx` (new) — renders a 4-column grid of
  the seeded images. Click selects; second click on the same image
  deselects. Selected state shows a coloured ring (use existing
  design tokens). Component is controlled: takes
  `value: string | null` and `onChange: (next: string | null) =>
  void`. Includes `data-testid="hero-image-picker"` and per-image
  testids `hero-image-option-${index}`.
- `components/HeroImagePicker.README.md` (new)
- `components/PostForm.tsx` (MODIFY — insert `<HeroImagePicker>` above
  the link-share section, below the body field. Wired to a new
  controlled state slice. On submit, includes `heroImageUrl` in the
  mutation input. Field labelled "Add a hero image (optional)".)

**Card + detail rendering:**

- `components/PostCard.tsx` (MODIFY — when `post.heroImageUrl` is
  present, render an `<img>` at the top of the card body, above the
  title. Aspect ratio 16:9, `object-fit: cover`, `border-radius`
  matching card. `loading="lazy"`. Falls back to no-image rendering
  when null. Keep existing card structure intact when
  `heroImageUrl === null`.)
- `app/post/[id]/page.tsx` (MODIFY — when `post.heroImageUrl` is
  present, render the image at the top of the detail content,
  larger than card (full content width, max-height ~480px). Keep
  page layout otherwise unchanged.)

**Seed data:**

- `prisma/seed.ts` (MODIFY — assign hero images to ~half of seeded
  posts, distributed across the 8 images so all are visually
  testable. Existing seed posts keep their other fields; new field
  populated only.)

**Tests:**

- `server/services/post.test.ts` (MODIFY or new) — `createPost` with
  a valid seeded URL succeeds; with an unseeded URL is rejected;
  `listPosts` returns `heroImageUrl` for posts that have one.
- `components/HeroImagePicker.test.tsx` (new) — click selects; second
  click deselects; renders all seeded images; testids present.

**README updates:**

- `components/README.md` (MODIFY — add `HeroImagePicker` to the
  component list)
- `server/services/post.README.md` (MODIFY if exists, ADD if not —
  note `heroImageUrl` field on create / list / get)

### Do NOT touch

- Any existing field on `Post` other than the additive
  `heroImageUrl` column. No reordering, no comments rewritten on
  unrelated fields.
- `linkImageUrl` and the link-card rendering (BU-link-share / D060
  scope). When both exist, hero wins for the top-of-card slot; the
  link card keeps its own thumbnail.
- `Reaction`, `Comment`, `Request` models and their flows.
- The composer's intent-picker logic (D062) and Send-for-Review
  flow (D063).
- Authentication, dispatch, vetting flows.
- Real upload infrastructure — no S3, no file inputs, no FormData,
  no multipart. The picker is grid-only.
- og:image fetching (BU-link-share) — that path stays exactly as is.

### Out of scope for this session

These are explicitly parked elsewhere and must NOT be built here:

- Real member upload (file input → upload → S3 → URL). Phase 2
  BU-image scope.
- Multiple images per post / carousel. Parking-lot "Multimedia
  composer".
- Editing hero image on existing posts. Parking-lot "Composer
  enhancements (Phase 2+)" / edit-post flow.
- og:image-as-hero auto-population from `linkUrl` (already in D046
  Phase MVP day 1 — different code path; do not touch).
- Bullet-list rendering in body. Parked separately.
- Reactions reveal-on-hover. Parked separately.
- Image moderation / EXIF strip / size limits / thumbnails. All
  Phase 2 BU-image scope; not relevant when source is a seeded
  fixed-bucket URL.

---

## Contracts

### Inputs consumed

- `Post` type from generated Prisma client (after migration)
- `SEED_HERO_IMAGES` from `shared/seed-images.ts` (new — this BU is
  its first consumer)
- Existing composer state shape in `components/PostForm.tsx`
- Existing tRPC `post.create` input type — gains one optional field

### Outputs produced

- `Post.heroImageUrl: string | null` — additive Post field
- `SEED_HERO_IMAGES` constant — the canonical demo bucket. Phase 2
  BU-image will deprecate this when real upload lands.
- `<HeroImagePicker>` component — used by `PostForm`. May be reused
  by future edit-post flow.

---

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `npx prisma migrate dev` runs cleanly; new column appears on Post
- [ ] `prisma seed` runs without error and populates hero images on
      ~half of seeded posts
- [ ] On `/compose`, `<HeroImagePicker>` renders 8 images in a grid
- [ ] Clicking an image selects it (visible ring); clicking again
      deselects
- [ ] Submitting the form with no image selected creates a post with
      `heroImageUrl: null` (existing path unaffected)
- [ ] Submitting with an image selected creates a post with the
      chosen URL stored
- [ ] Server validation rejects any `heroImageUrl` not in the seeded
      list (returns Zod error)
- [ ] On `/feed`, posts with hero images render the image at the top
      of the card; posts without are visually unchanged from before
- [ ] On `/post/<id>`, posts with hero images render the image larger
      at the top of the detail content
- [ ] `loading="lazy"` on card images; explicit `alt` text from
      `SEED_HERO_IMAGES[i].alt`
- [ ] No layout shift on cards without a hero (existing card height
      preserved when null)
- [ ] All tests pass; typecheck clean; lint clean
- [ ] Manual click-through: compose → pick image → publish → see in
      feed → click through to detail page → image renders consistently

---

## UI states

| State | Trigger | What user sees | What user can do |
|---|---|---|---|
| Picker — none selected | Default on form mount | 4×2 grid of 8 thumbnails, label "Add a hero image (optional)" | Click any thumbnail to select |
| Picker — one selected | After click | Selected thumbnail has a coloured ring; others dim slightly | Click selected to deselect, or click another to switch |
| Card — no hero | `heroImageUrl === null` | Existing card layout unchanged | — |
| Card — with hero | `heroImageUrl !== null` | Image at top of card body, 16:9, lazy-loaded | Click card → detail page |
| Detail — no hero | `heroImageUrl === null` | Existing detail layout unchanged | — |
| Detail — with hero | `heroImageUrl !== null` | Image at top of content, full content width, max-height ~480px | — |

---

## Tests required

- Unit: `<HeroImagePicker>` selection state machine
- Unit: server validation rejects unseeded URLs; accepts seeded
- Integration: createPost with hero → listPosts returns it →
  getPostById returns it
- Manual click-through (per acceptance list above)

Not required:

- Visual regression tests
- Performance benchmarks for image load (lazy-loading is enough)

---

## Scenarios to verify against

`docs/product/scenarios.md`:

- Scenario 1 (Sky News bias post) — post is text + AM URL today;
  manually click-through with a hero image to confirm it doesn't
  break the existing flow
- Scenario 7+ (urgent action templates per D044) — hero image is the
  natural shape for these; verify visually but don't change the
  scenarios

WhatsApp pattern reference (informal, no canonical scenario yet):

- The Asda action video — a real post in our target community with
  hero media. Demo image set should include at least one
  storefront-ish photo that approximates the genre.
- The Archway field-report photo — a "people with a banner" image.
  Demo set should include at least one of those.

---

## Known gotchas

- The seeded image URLs must be served from `/seed-images/<file>` by
  Next.js' static-asset path. Verify the URLs work from the deployed
  origin, not just local dev.
- `next/image` vs raw `<img>` — keep it simple with raw `<img>` for
  this BU. `next/image` adds Image Optimization which we don't need
  for fixed-bucket demo URLs and which adds config noise.
- The validator must check both schema (URL string) AND that the URL
  is in `SEED_HERO_IMAGES`. Otherwise members could pass any URL via
  the API — for demo this is a deliberate constraint, not laziness.
- Existing posts in the DB are unaffected by the migration — the new
  column is nullable. Don't backfill.
- `linkImageUrl` (BU-link-share) is a different field. Don't merge,
  don't reorder, don't repurpose.

---

## Definition of done

- [ ] All files in "Build" list created or modified; nothing in
      "Don't touch" list touched
- [ ] TypeScript compiles with zero errors, zero `any`,
      zero `@ts-ignore`
- [ ] All acceptance criteria verified
- [ ] Tests pass: `npm run test`
- [ ] Typecheck passes: `npm run typecheck`
- [ ] Lint passes: `npm run lint`
- [ ] Manual click-through completed (compose → publish → feed →
      detail)
- [ ] README files updated where touched
- [ ] No TODOs left in committed code
- [ ] Commit messages follow convention (`feat:` / `docs:` / etc.)
- [ ] PR description summarises changes, links D064 and this brief

---

## Open questions to surface

These should be raised back to Paul, not decided autonomously:

- **Hero vs link image priority on the card** — when a post has BOTH
  `heroImageUrl` and `linkImageUrl`, the brief assumes hero wins for
  top-of-card and the link card keeps its own thumbnail in its own
  card below. Confirm this is the intended layering.
- **Specific image set** — final selection of the 8 images. The brief
  describes the genre (generic, activist-friendly); actual JPGs need
  to be picked and committed. Source recommendations: Unsplash CC0
  search terms `protest`, `community`, `street`, `signs`. Surface
  the chosen set for sign-off before commit.
- **Card aspect ratio** — 16:9 is the proposed default. WhatsApp's
  posts are often 4:3 or square. If the demo images are mostly
  landscape, 16:9 is fine. If mixed, consider letting the natural
  aspect render with `object-fit: cover` and a fixed max-height.
